name: Genoise
recipeIngredient: 100 grams sweet butter, melted and clarified
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease and lightly flour one of the following pans:
- two 9-inch layer cake tins
- three 7-inch layer cake tins
- one 11x16 jelly-roll pan
- two shallow 10-inch layer-cake tins
2. Preheat oven to 80°C.
3. In a large bowl, combine eggs and sugar. Stir for about a minute, or until they are just combined. Set the bowl over a saucepan containing water (about 3-5 cm deep). The water should not touch the bowl or boil. Place the saucepan with the bowl over low heat for 5-10 minutes, or until the eggs feel lukewarm. Stir occasionally to prevent them from cooking at the bottom of the bowl.
4. When the eggs are lukewarm and appear like bright yellow syrup, remove the bowl from the heat. Beat the mixture at high speed for 10-15 minutes, scraping with a rubber spatula when necessary, until it becomes light, fluffy, and cool (it will almost triple in volume and look like whipped cream).
5. Sprinkle flour over the beaten eggs, adding cooled clarified butter and vanilla gently. Mix by hand or using an electric mixer on the lowest speed. Be careful not to overmix.
6. Pour batter into prepared pans. Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until cakes lift easily from the sides of the pan and feel springy when touched on top.
7. Remove from pans immediately and cool on a cake rack.
Variations:
To make lemon or orange genoise, add 1 teaspoon grated orange or lemon rind and 2 tablespoons orange or lemon juice to the melted butter before adding it to the batter.
For genoise for petits fours, increase flour to 180 grams and bake in an 30x40 cm jelly-roll pan.
For nutted genoise, add 75 grams finely grated nuts when incorporating the flour.
Difficulty: Moderate
Precision: Measure ingredients accurately.
